steps.title: Calculate buildings on cropland as a percentage of the total property
steps.content:
Cropland comprises 56% of the total property. Buildings exist on 22.5% of the cropland. Therefore, the area with buildings on cropland is:
$$22.5\% \times 56\% = 0.225 \times 0.56 = 0.126 = 12.6\%$$
So 12.6% of the total property has buildings on the cropland.

steps.title: Calculate buildings on meadow as a percentage of the total property
steps.content:
Meadow comprises the remaining portion: $100\% - 56\% = 44\%$ of the property. Buildings exist on 12.5% of the meadow. Therefore:
$$12.5\% \times 44\% = 0.125 \times 0.44 = 0.055 = 5.5\%$$
So 5.5% of the total property has buildings on the meadow.

steps.title: Sum the percentages to find p
steps.content:
The total percentage of the property with buildings is the sum of buildings on cropland and buildings on meadow:
$$p = 12.6\% + 5.5\% = 18.1\%$$
Therefore, $p = 18.1$.
